title: Multi-thread depth on enterprise deals doubled since Q1
anchor: Active opportunities above $250K ACV now average 4.2 engaged contacts, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1.
connect: Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% versus 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
body: Active opportunities above $250K ACV now average 4.2 engaged contacts, up from 2.1 contacts in Q1. Win rate at 4+ contacts runs at 41% versus 18% for deals with 1-2 contacts.
trace: Multi-thread depth signal from $250K+ deals showing Q1 to Q2 improvement correlated with win rate variance by contact count.

title: Sterling deal expanded buying committee to seven contacts post-demo
anchor: Executive demo on April 18th brought CFO and business-unit leads into the evaluation, growing from 4 to 7 contacts.
connect: Committees that grow during evaluation close at 26% higher rates than flat committees.
body: Executive demo on April 18th brought CFO and business-unit leads into the evaluation, growing from 4 to 7 contacts. Committees that grow during evaluation close at 26% higher rates than flat committees.
trace: Sterling enterprise deal showing committee expansion pattern after executive demo, with historical benchmark on growing committees.

title: Champion re-engagement drives stage movement within three weeks
anchor: Nine of 14 deals advanced a stage within 21 days of champion re-engagement in April.
connect: Champion contact lapses logged on April 14th and March 6th preceded the re-engagement cycle.

title: Q3 pipeline coverage sits at 3.4x quota with live engagement at 79%

title: Proposal-stage aging clears with re-issued documents

title: Mid-market win rate climbed 8 points with ICP alignment driving the lift

connect: Q2 mid-market volume was 70 deals versus 68 in Q1.
